lede: A look at February 12 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Khvicha Kvaratskhelia and Carlton Brewster, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2026 Bangladeshi general election
events.desc: In the first general election since the July Revolution in Bangladesh, the Bangladesh Nationalist Party, led by Tarique Rahman, wins a landslide victory upon returning to power after almost 19 years. A referendum held alongside was also approved by the majority of voters.

events.title: National Gallery (Norway)
events.desc: Four thieves break into the National Gallery of Norway and steal Edvard Munch's iconic painting The Scream.

events.title: Murder of James Bulger
events.desc: Two-year-old James Bulger is abducted from New Strand Shopping Centre by two ten-year-old boys, who later torture and murder him.

events.title: Gateway Arch
events.desc: Construction begins on the Gateway Arch in St. Louis, Missouri.

events.title: Tornado outbreak of February 12, 1945
events.desc: A devastating tornado outbreak in Mississippi and Alabama kills 45 people and injures 427 others.

events.title: USS Macon (ZRS-5)
events.desc: USS Macon, one of the two largest helium-filled airships ever created, crashes into the Pacific Ocean off the coast of California and sinks.

events.title: Puyi
events.desc: The Xuantong Emperor, the last Emperor of China, abdicates.

events.title: SS Penguin
events.desc: New Zealand's worst maritime disaster of the 20th century happens when the SS Penguin, an inter-island ferry, sinks and explodes at the entrance to Wellington Harbour.

events.title: Georgia Day
events.desc: Georgia Day: Englishman James Oglethorpe founds Georgia, the 13th colony of the Thirteen Colonies, by settling at Savannah.

events.title: Isabella I of Castile
events.desc: Isabella I issues an edict outlawing Islam in the Crown of Castile, forcing virtually all her Muslim subjects to convert to Christianity.

deaths.text: Ivan Reitman, Slovak-Canadian actor, director, and producer
George Heriot, Scottish goldsmith and philanthropist, founded George Heriot's School
